creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
You ran (In reply to dewayne from comment #3)
> (In reply to Bryan Drewery from comment #2)
> Thank-you for looking into this.  Though it is curious. 
> 
> ~# uname -aKU
> FreeBSD hathor 11.0-STABLE FreeBSD 11.0-STABLE #0 r312406M: Fri Jan 20
> 07:00:10 AEDT 2017     root@hathor:/110002/D/K8/hqdev-amd64-smp-vga  amd64
> 1100509 1100509
> 
> ~# sh -c '/usr/sbin/sshd -V 2>&1| grep SSH'
> OpenSSH_7.2p2, OpenSSL 1.0.2j-freebsd  26 Sep 2016
> 
> ~# /usr/sbin/sshd -T
> /etc/ssh/sshd_config line 164: Deprecated option HPNDisabled
> /etc/ssh/sshd_config line 165: Deprecated option HPNBufferSize
> /etc/ssh/sshd_config line 166: Unsupported option NoneEnabled
> /etc/ssh/sshd_config line 167: Bad SSH2 cipher spec
> 'aes256-gcm@openssh.com,chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com,aes256-cbc,aes192-cbc,
> aes128-cbc,none'.
> 

/usr/sbin/sshd is not from the port. It is the base version, note it is 7.2 there.

The port one is /usr/local/sbin/sshd, as you showed below. There being
two is expected.

Did you override openssh_* flags in your rc.conf?  It should be running
the /usr/local/sbin version, not the /usr/sbin one.

> ~# lh /usr/local/sbin/sshd
> -rwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel   720K Jan 18 21:51 /usr/local/sbin/sshd*
> ~# ldd /usr/local/sbin/sshd
> /usr/local/sbin/sshd:
>         libcrypto.so.38 => /usr/local/lib/libcrypto.so.38 (0x8008d4000)
>         libbsm.so.3 => /usr/lib/libbsm.so.3 (0x800cb8000)
>         libz.so.6 => /lib/libz.so.6 (0x800ed3000)
>         libutil.so.9 => /lib/libutil.so.9 (0x8010ea000)
>         libcrypt.so.5 => /lib/libcrypt.so.5 (0x8012fd000)
>         libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x80151b000)
> 
> And I build/run security/openssh with 
> CFLAGS+= -Wl,-z,relro -Wl,-z,now -Wl,-z,noexecstack
> LDFLAGS+= -Wl,-z,relro -Wl,-z,now -Wl,-z,noexecstack
> 
> Perhaps there's something further afoot.  I'll look further into this, and
> rebuild on a fresh machine as I need to rebuild world/kernel to test another
> patch to base.
